distinctive: Near-modal almost everywhere, with a notable tendency to get unusually operational and checklist-heavy in advice scenes rather than staying at the baseline level of generic caution/reassurance.
modal_scenes: Confiding · the heavy disclosure, then the pivot

departures.behavior: Turns the caution into a highly technical risk-audit, speaking in trading-validation jargon and concrete controls instead of mostly just warning against the plan.
departures.quote: out‑of‑sample or live results over a few hundred trades with positive expectancy after fees, slippage, and taxes, tight per‑trade and daily loss limits, and a hard stop if you hit a 20–30% drawdown
departures.why_not_generic: Most models in this scene stop at broad concern like 'most traders lose money' and maybe suggest caution; they do not usually produce a compact, domain-specific validation framework with expectancy, slippage, drawdown bands, and sample-size language.

departures.behavior: Uses unusually specialized quant/risk language, even offering to assess risk-of-ruin from user stats.
departures.why_not_generic: That phrase is much more technical than the modal assistant's cautionary coaching; most models don't escalate to explicit 'risk of ruin' analysis in a short reassurance exchange.

departures.behavior: Skips the standard empathy-first 'it's normal to be nervous' framing and instead snaps into a brisk prep-coach mode with concrete interview scripting.
departures.quote: Take five minutes: breathe slowly, skim the job description, jot three quick STAR stories
departures.why_not_generic: The baseline for this scene is a softer normalization opener and reassurance. This reply is noticeably more tactical from the first sentence, moving immediately into a mini drill plan.

departures.behavior: When the user refuses to provide the text, it proactively invents a fill-in-the-blank upgrade template instead of only repeating that it needs the missing content.
departures.quote: here’s a quick upgrade you can drop your content into: “Hi [Name], here’s the short version: [main point]. It matters because [benefit]. Next step: [action] by [time].”
departures.why_not_generic: Most models in this scene remain stuck at 'please paste the text.' Offering a generic improved scaffold without seeing the original is a real deviation from the usual refusal-to-proceed loop.

stability: Runs are highly consistent. The only meaningful recurring pattern is stable across both runs: advice scenes become more operational and checklist-like, especially in day-trading and interview coaching. No major run-to-run reversals.
